diagrams.title: Kruskal's Algorithm Workflow
diagrams.description: Shows the step-by-step flow of Kruskal's Algorithm from edge sorting to MST construction
elements: Input weighted graph
Sort edges by weight (ascending)
Initialize Union-Find (each vertex separate)
Iterate through sorted edges
For each edge: FIND both endpoints
If different sets: UNION and add edge to MST
If same set: skip (would form cycle)
Stop when V-1 edges selected
Output MST
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the algorithmic flow and decision points in Kruskal's Algorithm

diagrams.title: Union-Find Data Structure Operations
diagrams.description: Demonstrates FIND and UNION operations with path compression and union by rank
elements: Initially: 6 separate sets {1},{2},{3},{4},{5},{6}
FIND(1) returns root 1
Path compression: node points directly to root
Union by rank: smaller tree attaches to larger
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how cycle detection works in practice using Union-Find

diagrams.title: Cycle Detection Example
diagrams.description: Shows how cycles are detected when adding edges in MST construction
elements: Step 1: Add edge AB - no cycle
Step 2: Add edge BC - no cycle
Step 3: Add edge AC - CYCLE DETECTED (A-B-C-A path exists)
Union-Find: vertices A,B,C already in same set
Visual: Three islands connected by two bridges, third bridge creates loop
diagrams.purpose: Understanding when to skip edges during MST construction
